sentences.text: Hm.
Hello.
Hey
Veronica, how are you doing?
Good.
How are you doing?
I'm doing pretty well.
I'm happy.
It's Friday.
Long weekend coming up.
So,
yeah, how are you doing?
Good.
Thank you.
Sorry about, uh, yesterday or whatever day it was.
I can't even remember
now.
Not a problem,
not a problem at all.
Glad you're here.
Um, yeah, I was talking with Anna though.
She was kind of filling me in on some of your conversations, um, curious how you heard about apartment IQ or what you know about us.
So, actually I hadn't, she reached out to me and I don't remember.
Um, I feel like we may have had a conversation a while back at some point, but I don't really know much.
Um, we just had agreed to.
So when she happened to call me to schedule this call, um, I was actually off and in Utah, um, with my family, um, for, uh, like a family emergency if you will.

And, uh, um, I happened to answer because we had to answer all unknown numbers and I happened to answer, um, uh, and it was her and so I was happy to schedule the appointment but we didn't get a lot of opportunity to talk just because I, I was not in a place where I could do that.
Um, so anyway, that's kind of where this meeting has come
from.
Makes sense.
Are you, are you in southern California now, or where are you based out of?
No, I'm based out of Idaho actually.
Oh, very nice.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So our corporate office, the owners there in California and then, um the main hub of Capstone is based out of Idaho.
Um, we have um, six properties here in Idaho and then we have some in Arizona and uh a new development that's happening now in California.
Very cool.
Then we have one in Texas.
Ok, cool.
Yeah, I'm down in, uh, in, in Phoenix.
So just kind of, I guess.
But, yeah, I try to make it up to Utah as much as possible.
I've been to Sun Valley skiing one time.
I, I love both Utah and uh in Idaho, that's for sure.
Nice, nice.
Um, yeah, actually our asset director is based out of Arizona.
Thanks.
Yeah,
very cool.
Well, yeah, I'll give you kind of the high level of us.
It's, it's a pretty simple conversation.
Uh We're a B I tool.
We provide you with market rate information on all of your competitors.
Um There's four things that make our product really unique.
So, first off, this is a fully automated experience anytime that your team needs comp data.
You can literally just jump into our app and see what's going on.
Um We also update our data every single day.
So as, as I'm sure you're aware, you know, properties out there are changing rates regularly.

So for you to be able to jump in again and see what's going on today rather than last week or last month, uh, we can also track any property out there as long as they have a website and use a property management software.
They don't have to be our customers whatsoever.
So right now we're tracking almost 70,000 properties and almost in over 17 million units across the country every single night.
So coverage everywhere.
Um, all markets, ter sharing, markets, et cetera.
And I will, I do like to mention to you just in light of the kind of like the real page lawsuit.
Now, the yard lawsuit centered around the sharing of private data, everything that we have inside of apartment IQ is 100% publicly available information.
So nothing to do with those, those um, lawsuits that are going on right now.
Ok.
Um I immediately wondered how you could, uh, do that if they weren't uh
uh me our customers of yours.
Yep.
Good question.
So we actually build a tool.
It goes up the property websites and we get all of the data directly from the property websites and more specifically from the online leasing section on the property website.
So that's why I was saying it for us to track a competitor.
They do have to have a website and use a property management software.
Gotcha.
Yep.
Yeah.
So everything we show in, in an apartment IQ is a reflection of what a renter would see if they visit your site or visit a competitor's website.
So, yeah.
What are you guys doing right now?
As far as market surveys or comp data?
It goes.
yeah.
So we have a uh our own uh market survey that we do.
And so we have the on site teams um collect all of the information whether you know, online, uh speaking with the actual comps or um shopping them, going there and, and shopping them.

But um they're collecting all the data and then we have um separate meetings with the ownership group where we kind of analyze all the and um you know, have our discussions and then go back to them with whatever decision has to be made based off that information.
Nice.
Makes sense.
Um Are you guys pricing your units internally then or do you use a rev management tool?
I guess
we do, we do it, we do it internal.
Nice.
Very cool.
Well, yeah, I mean, I think what we could do is is probably give you better data to take back to the team to, to make those pricing decisions.
Um Obviously the issue with kind of the call arounds is, is hoping somebody answers the phone and then two hoping they give you, you know, good, good information.
They don't make something.
So I think that's one of the value props.
But yeah, let me walk you through the platform and, and show you what we got.
Yeah, sounds great.
Cool.
You ever see my screen?
Ok,
nice.
So this is what your apartment IQ landing page would look like uh each one of your properties would actually get their own individual property card.
And then all of the data is based off of that property specific concept.
Uh I will let you know that your team is always in complete control of your comp set.
So typically what happens whenever we set up a new customer is that they provide us with a list of comps that they've been tracking and we start there.
However, we do also have a comp matching algorithm where essentially what we do is we look at every property in the vicinity of your property and we let you know how well it comps out.
So some recommendations are helpful in those markets where there's a lot of new inventory coming on board or perhaps uh perhaps renovations.
So just making sure you're tracking everybody that is relevant.
But nonetheless, like I said, you're in control and you can always add or remove a property to your comp set directly inside the app itself as well.
OK.
Yeah, I will, I will also mention that we offer unlimited users.

So a lot of our customers do like to give access to their on site teams, regional managers, you know, marketing, whoever you wanna give access to even even some of your uh leadership team or investors.
If you wanna give them access, you can no cap there.
Um We do a ton of uh a ton of training as well.
So group sessions, one on one sessions, ongoing training sessions as you get new employees.
But yeah, we wanna make sure whoever, whoever's utilizing the data is comfortable navigating the platform.

But yeah, like I said, each property gets its own card and then to view the analytics for the property, you click the view button and it'll take you inside the data once you're in there at the very top, this will be your main navigation.
So each property is four reports associated with it.
The first report which we're on here is the market survey.
And then if you select the data drop down, you'll see three additional reports as well.
Uh Just starting with our market survey though, you know, very simple, very straightforward.
We simply list all of your competitors across the top and then we also include your property which would be highlighted here in the blue.
But our goal is to make it as easy as possible to compare your property's performance versus the competition.
Um There are three different sections here.
So property summary being a lot of that high level property data, some performance based analytics.
Um You know, we'll show you things like your built total number of units um advertised occupancy percentage as you can see exposure percentage.
Um We'll give you some transactional data as well.

So the number of leases that each property has had over the last 30 days, the last seven days and the number of leases each property does average per week as well as obviously letting you know if there is a rent special or concession tied to the property or tied to a specific unit in the property.

And what's really cool here with concessions too is we actually take it a step further and we'll calculate net effective rent and net effective price per foot for you at the property level at the floor plan level and all the way down to the unit level.
So very helpful in those concession heavy markets.
Um What's nice too is all of this data is sort from low to high and high to low.
So if you want to see which property in your comps that has the most units available again, you can sort this from high to low and you can see the residents of W photos first.
Our property has the fifth most.
Got you some nice functionality there.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And the second section is pricing and availability.
So this is broken out by floor plan.
And the idea here is to essentially understand, you know, where do our one bedrooms fit in comparison to the competition.
And inside of here, I think we give you every key metric to help understand that.
So we'll show you the gross rent again.
We'll let you know if there's a concession attached to the specific floor plan, we'll show you net effective rent square footage price per foot, net effective price per foot.
And the number of one bedrooms at each property is advertising is available.
So whether your team likes to base performance off of rent or price per foot, again, you can sort it from high to low.
You can see as the most expensive one bedrooms, who is the least expensive one bedrooms.
And ultimately, you know, is this where you this this is where you're positioned.
So is that in line with your strategy, is your team worried about occupancy?
Are you looking to push rent rates?

Um even things too like understanding the context of concessions and some of these properties are offering a full month free rent whereas other ones are offering a half month and a half month and even just 100 and $50 off.
Mhm Also to understanding kind of their strategy based off of their their exposure so that you know, we only have 71 bedrooms available.
These guys have 12 and 13 1 bedrooms available.
So double the units in us, they're probably a little bit more inclined to get some of those uh rented even if it is at a lower price.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And then fees and deposits as well.
So we'll let you know security fees, admin, fees, a fees, parking, pet information.
So, yeah, like I said, just automated, uh, data updated every single day you jump into here, you see what's going on.

Um, what's nice too is we have a report that, uh, if, if you want to send to some of the investors or some of the senior leaders, um these things can be downloaded if frequently officer you like, you can also automate them to be delivered to their inbox on whatever cadence you'd like.
But they're pretty sharp, they're uh they're property specific.
We do time stamp them down here in the bottom left.
So whoever is looking at the data is on the same page as far as whatever's been pulled, then you get some high level market summary information, some high level property insights.
And we also give a unit type analysis for floor plan that is available in your comp set right now.
Got you.
Yeah, that's pretty cool.
Yeah, I mean that's kind of the high level like I said, and we can get your dad all the way down to the unit level and we can show you things like

we can show you things like uh unit number, floor plan, name days on market at the unit level again, concessions, net effective rent, net effective price per foot at the unit level so we can, uh, we can definitely, you know, in terms of, of, of pricing your units, we can make sure you're doing it appropriately based on, off of what the market is dictating.
So.
Ok.
Yeah, I like it.
Yeah,
pretty cool.
Makes, makes life a little bit easier.
Um, now I'll let you know too as far as pricing goes.
So we charge per unit per month, the more of your units you bring on board, uh the more incentivized we can make the rate.
So um how many units do you guys have in the in the portfolio total?
And I can get you an idea for price?
Um So we're just a little over um 800 here in Idaho and then in Arizona we're about 1000.
Um I don't have the exact number for Arizona and then um those are the only ones we manage right now.
Um When California comes on board, then obviously we'll manage that one.
We don't manage.
It's third party managed at the moment.
Yeah, then that's across maybe 10 or 14 properties.
Um Yeah, it's across.
So we have 67899 unit nine properties,
right?
Yeah, so I'll show you I'm gonna try to pull up my rate card.
I got it.
Yeah, I got too many windows open.
So for us.
So for us, if you, if you're under 2000 units units should be a dollar per unit per month.
Ok.
Yeah,
and we do charge, we do charge a set up fee, but I'm happy to waive those for you.
Ok.
Yeah, I'd be happy to share this um with our asset director and then she'll, you know, share it if she feels it's appropriate with the ownership group.
Um because we have had obviously as you know, with the market and how things are we have been, have we just revamped our market survey um template that we're utilizing as a matter of fact.
Um Those conversations are so um um important.
I mean, they always have been, but especially focused on now um that we are having um uh reviews of those with the ownership directly um every month.
And I mean, obviously we do market service as often as needed, but at least once a month that we submit to the ownership group.

And so, um we definitely, it's been uh a hot topic as I'm sure it is for everyone um at this time especially, but um I'd be happy to uh mention it to them if you have something you can send me uh she's on vacation and won't be back until the end of the beginning of the, of June.
Um So then I can touch base with her and see if it's something that they want to entertain or not.
But I think it's a great uh program.
It would be very helpful and I felt like it would save a lot of time.
Yeah.
I'll, um, I'll send you over some collateral at the end of the call here.
Um, obviously if she or anybody else would like, uh, a walk through, I'm happy to do it.
Uh, one thing I'll let you know too.
What, what's kind of crazy.
I, I've got a list of 11 of the top 30 PM CS in the country that are no longer allowing their site teams to participate in color on market surveys anymore.
It's actually stemming from the lawsuit.
So I think that information is going to be harder and harder to get.
So the automated solution that we offer is nice from that perspective.
But yeah, let me send you over some information.
Um I can follow up in the beginning of June then if I don't hear from you prior, that would be great.
Yeah, obviously if you have any questions, definitely let me know otherwise have a great holiday weekend and we'll talk soon.
Thank you.
You too.
Thanks.
You too.
Bye.
